content.text:
Parent conversation handoff summary:
Parent conversation handoff summary
Summary model: openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini
Source items: 73

Overview:
The discussion centered on whether LightRAG-style simplification would fit this repo’s AI stack, then shifted into a broader AI pipeline optimization effort. The repo already has a fairly advanced RAG/AI architecture: Spring Boot backend, Pinecone vector search, prompt templates, eval harnesses, and derived read models. The resulting direction was to keep a reusable experiment framework, on-demand A/B evaluation, and a few quality/cost improvements, all with backward-compatible defaults.

Settled decisions:
- Proceed with a reusable live experiment framework, not just one-off eval scripts.
- Keep experiments default-off so current behavior is unchanged unless explicitly enabled.
- Use OpenRouter as the sole LLM gateway; do not add direct provider calls.
- Use existing eval datasets/judging infrastructure for on-demand A/B comparisons.
- Record experiment/model metadata in existing audit-related fields rather than adding schema migrations.
- Add prompt caching support for Anthropic models via OpenRouter.
- Add SQL routing for analytical questions to use read models instead of vector search.
- Keep HyDE as an opt-in, experiment-controlled RAG enhancement.
- Add chunk-size monitoring/analysis as a diagnostic/observability improvement.
- A plan file was written: `duet-plan-ai-optimization.md` for duet-based implementation.

Constraints / must-remember items:
- Implementation should stay backend-only for this plan; no frontend/package/e2e changes.
- Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.4 stack; avoid introducing new framework dependencies.
- All new behaviors must be backward compatible and disabled by default.
- OpenRouter remains the only model gateway.
- Existing eval tests must continue to pass.
- The plan assumes no database schema changes.
- The repo already contains derived read models relevant to SQL routing: UserWeekFact, TeamWeekRollup, RcdoWeekRollup, CarryForwardFact, ComplianceFact.
- Duet config in `.pi/duet/config.json` shows static checks: `npm run check:static`; unit checks: `npm test`.
- There is already an in-progress duet run with modified files under `.pi/duet/runs/2026-03-29T18-32-40-613Z-32faa966/` and tracked changes in `AiController` plus new plan recommendation files.

Open questions:
- Should the initial implementation keep the full live traffic experiment service, or trim it down later if it proves too heavy?
- Which exact models should be treated as control/treatment defaults for LLM and embeddings in the reusable A/B harness?
- Should analytical SQL routing be limited to clearly aggregate/comparison queries, or should it also handle broader reporting-style questions?
- How much observability is wanted for prompt caching and chunk-size analysis beyond basic logging/counters?
- Should the A/B runner compare only LLMs initially, or also embeddings and HyDE in the same reusable harness?

Use this handoff as background context. If it conflicts with the explicit task below or the current repository state, prioritize the explicit task and repo state.

Plan overview:
{
  "goal": "Add seven optimization capabilities to the AI pipeline — prompt caching, A/B testing infrastructure, embedding model override (offline eval only), LLM model A/B, HyDE, SQL query routing, and chunk size analysis — all backward-compatible and default-off. The embedding-model A/B experiment is scoped to offline evaluation only (via AbComparisonRunner) because the Pinecone index is fixed at 1536 dimensions matching text-embedding-3-small; routing live queries through text-embedding-3-large (3072-d) would cause dimension mismatches. Backend-only; no frontend, e2e, or schema changes.",
  "totalSteps": 7,
  "stepTitles": [
    "1. Prompt Caching in OpenRouterAiProvider",
    "2. A/B Testing Infrastructure",
    "3. Embedding Model Override (Offline Eval) + Chunk Size Analysis",
    "4. LLM Model A/B + Experiment-Aware Provider",
    "5. HyDE Service + SQL Query Router",
    "6. Wire HyDE, SQL Routing, and LLM A/B into SemanticQueryService",
    "7. A/B Comparison Eval Runner"
  ]
}

Step 1: Prompt Caching in OpenRouterAiProvider
{
  "id": "step-1",
  "title": "Prompt Caching in OpenRouterAiProvider",
  "description": "Add Anthropic prompt caching support to OpenRouterAiProvider. In callOpenRouter(), when building the messages array, if model.startsWith(\"anthropic/\"), add a cache_control object {\"type\": \"ephemeral\"} to the system message JSON object so the system message becomes {\"role\": \"system\", \"content\": \"...\", \"cache_control\": {\"type\": \"ephemeral\"}}. Parse the response usage object for cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens fields and expose via AtomicLong counters (following the existing totalTokensUsed / totalRequests pattern). Log cache metrics at DEBUG level. Add an INFO log on construction: \"Prompt caching enabled for Anthropic models\". Files to modify: backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/OpenRouterAiProvider.java. Files to modify for tests: backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/OpenRouterAiProviderTest.java — add tests verifying cache metric counters are parsed from response usage and that counters default to zero. Note: the cache_control annotation in callOpenRouter() currently uses this.model; Step 4 will refactor to use an effectiveModel parameter, at which point the cache_control check must use whichever model is actually in use.",
  "requiredChecks": [
    "static",
    "unit"
  ]
}

This is relay round 2. Another agent has already worked on this step.

You are in VERIFICATION MODE. Your default action is to APPROVE.
Focus your review on the DIFF — the changes made in the previous round.
Only make changes if there is a MATERIAL defect: failing check, missing step requirement, logic bug, or security issue.
Style preferences, alternative approaches, comment wording, and optional polish are NOT grounds for changes.

If all checks pass and the step requirements are met, approve immediately.
Log anything else you notice as observations — do not act on them.

Previous agent's notes:
Everything looks good. Let me provide a summary of all the changes made.

## Summary

### Changes Made

**`OpenRouterAiProvider.java`:**
1. **New counters**: Added `cacheCreationTokens` and `cacheReadTokens` `AtomicLong` fields.
2. **Constructor INFO log**: Added `log.info("Prompt caching enabled for Anthropic models")` unconditionally.
3. **`callOpenRouter()` — system message**: Replaced the one-liner `messages.addObject().put(...)` chain with separate variable construction. When `model.startsWith("anthropic/")`, adds `"cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"}` to the system message object.
4. **`callOpenRouter()` — usage parsing**: Delegated to new `updateUsageCounters(JsonNode usage)` package-private method.
5. **`updateUsageCounters(JsonNode usage)`**: New package-private method that parses `total_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens` from the usage node, updates all three counters, and logs cache fields at DEBUG level.
6. **New public getters**: `getCacheCreationTokens()` and `getCacheReadTokens()`.

**`OpenRouterAiProviderTest.java`:**
- Added `cacheCounters_startAtZero()` — verifies defaults to zero.
- Added `updateUsageCounters_withCacheFields_updatesCacheCounters()` — verifies both cache fields are parsed and counters updated.
- Added `updateUsageCounters_withoutCacheFields_cacheCountersRemainZero()` — verifies only `total_tokens` is updated when cache fields absent.
- Added `updateUsageCounters_calledMultipleTimes_accumulatesCounters()` — verifies accumulation across multiple calls.
- Added `updateUsageCounters_nullNode_doesNothing()` — verifies null-safety guard.

All checks pass: `static` ✅, `unit` ✅.

Verdict: changes_made

content.thinking:
**Investigating potential defects**

I need to determine if there's a material defect in the system. I think about possibly conducting targeted backend tests to see the step requirements. It seems the tests might not be enough, or they could be missing the actual request JSON for cache control. I should look into the callOpenRouter to check if the default test model isn't anthropic and if there are no tests for the system message. 

However, the requirement only specifies tests that check counters. I should make sure both unit tests and static tests pass, and I might want to consider logging information in the constructor, especially regarding prompt caching for anthropic models. There’s also a mention of exposing certain fields via AtomicLong counters, which might follow an existing pattern.

**Analyzing potential defects**

I’m identifying a potential material defect regarding how cache fields handle text versus numbers. If `asLong` returns 0, that might be okay, but I’ll need to check. There’s also a question about whether the debug log should include cumulative data per response, which seems important.

The callOpenRouter adds `cache_control` if the model starts with anthropic, but there's a note that this will change to use an `effectiveModel` parameter later. I should also consider that `totalRequests` might only increment on successful parsing, and I wonder about possible issues with Spotbugs. 

Lastly, I think about the git status and any pre-existing warnings, but ultimately, the unit and static tests passed with no changes. Observations to consider!

**Documenting observations**

I should consider noting any pre-existing warnings from unrelated files, but only if they come up outside the current step's scope. Since we noticed some in the frontend tests and linting, it might be worth logging these as observations, though I should keep it low-key if they aren't significant.

Summary model: openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini
Source items: 73

Overview:
The discussion centered on whether LightRAG-style simplification would fit this repo’s AI stack, then shifted into a broader AI pipeline optimization effort. The repo already has a fairly advanced RAG/AI architecture: Spring Boot backend, Pinecone vector search, prompt templates, eval harnesses, and derived read models. The resulting direction was to keep a reusable experiment framework, on-demand A/B evaluation, and a few quality/cost improvements, all with backward-compatible defaults.

Settled decisions:
- Proceed with a reusable live experiment framework, not just one-off eval scripts.
- Keep experiments default-off so current behavior is unchanged unless explicitly enabled.
- Use OpenRouter as the sole LLM gateway; do not add direct provider calls.
- Use existing eval datasets/judging infrastructure for on-demand A/B comparisons.
- Record experiment/model metadata in existing audit-related fields rather than adding schema migrations.
- Add prompt caching support for Anthropic models via OpenRouter.
- Add SQL routing for analytical questions to use read models instead of vector search.
- Keep HyDE as an opt-in, experiment-controlled RAG enhancement.
- Add chunk-size monitoring/analysis as a diagnostic/observability improvement.
- A plan file was written: `duet-plan-ai-optimization.md` for duet-based implementation.

Constraints / must-remember items:
- Implementation should stay backend-only for this plan; no frontend/package/e2e changes.
- Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.4 stack; avoid introducing new framework dependencies.
- All new behaviors must be backward compatible and disabled by default.
- OpenRouter remains the only model gateway.
- Existing eval tests must continue to pass.
- The plan assumes no database schema changes.
- The repo already contains derived read models relevant to SQL routing: UserWeekFact, TeamWeekRollup, RcdoWeekRollup, CarryForwardFact, ComplianceFact.
- Duet config in `.pi/duet/config.json` shows static checks: `npm run check:static`; unit checks: `npm test`.
- There is already an in-progress duet run with modified files under `.pi/duet/runs/2026-03-29T18-32-40-613Z-32faa966/` and tracked changes in `AiController` plus new plan recommendation files.

Open questions:
- Should the initial implementation keep the full live traffic experiment service, or trim it down later if it proves too heavy?
- Which exact models should be treated as control/treatment defaults for LLM and embeddings in the reusable A/B harness?
- Should analytical SQL routing be limited to clearly aggregate/comparison queries, or should it also handle broader reporting-style questions?
- How much observability is wanted for prompt caching and chunk-size analysis beyond basic logging/counters?
- Should the A/B runner compare only LLMs initially, or also embeddings and HyDE in the same reusable harness?

Use this handoff as background context. If it conflicts with the explicit task below or the current repository state, prioritize the explicit task and repo state.

  "goal": "Add seven optimization capabilities to the AI pipeline — prompt caching, A/B testing infrastructure, embedding model override (offline eval only), LLM model A/B, HyDE, SQL query routing, and chunk size analysis — all backward-compatible and default-off. The embedding-model A/B experiment is scoped to offline evaluation only (via AbComparisonRunner) because the Pinecone index is fixed at 1536 dimensions matching text-embedding-3-small; routing live queries through text-embedding-3-large (3072-d) would cause dimension mismatches. Backend-only; no frontend, e2e, or schema changes.",

ExperimentDefinition is a nested static class (mutable bean for Spring binding) with: boolean enabled, double controlWeight (0.0–1.0, default 1.0), String controlValue, String treatmentValue, List<String> appliesTo. (2) backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/experiment/ExperimentService.java — @Service with methods: assign(experimentName, userId) returns ExperimentAssignment record (experimentName, variant, value) using env var override AB_FORCE_{EXPERIMENT_NAME_UPPER}=control|treatment or Math.random() < controlWeight; isEnabled(experimentName); resolveValue(experimentName, userId); getActiveAssignments(userId) returns Map of all active assignments. (3) Modify backend/src/main/resources/application.yml — add under existing ai: key: ai.experiments.experiments map with three entries: embedding-model (enabled: false, control-weight: 1.0, control-value: openai/text-embedding-3-small, treatment-value: openai/text-embedding-3-large), llm-model (enabled: false, control-weight: 1.0, control-value: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4, treatment-value: google/gemini-2.5-flash-preview, applies-to: [COMMIT_LINT, RAG_INTENT, RAG_QUERY]), hyde (enabled: false, control-weight: 1.0, control-value: disabled, treatment-value: enabled, applies-to: [RAG_QUERY]). Note: embedding-model experiment is for offline eval only (AbComparisonRunner in step 7) — it must NOT be used in the live query path because the Pinecone index dimension is fixed at 1536. control-weight: 1.0 ensures all traffic defaults to control. ## Summary

### Files Created

**`ExperimentAssignment.java`** — Java record holding `(experimentName, variant, value)` with `isControl()` / `isTreatment()` convenience methods. Referenced by both `ExperimentService` and the tests.

**`ExperimentConfig.java`** — `@Configuration @ConfigurationProperties(prefix="ai.experiments")` class with a `Map<String, ExperimentDefinition> experiments` field. `ExperimentDefinition` is a mutable nested static bean with `enabled`, `controlWeight`, `controlValue`, `treatmentValue`, and `appliesTo` fields — all with proper getters/setters for Spring binding.

**`ExperimentService.java`** — `@Service` with:
- `isEnabled(experimentName)` — checks map and enabled flag
- `assign(experimentName, userId)` — priority: env-var override → random draw via `controlWeight`; disabled/missing experiments always return control
- `resolveValue(experimentName, userId)` — convenience wrapper returning just the value string  
- `getActiveAssignments(userId)` — iterates all experiments, returns map of only enabled ones
- `readForceOverride(experimentName)` — package-private (for spy-based testing); reads `AB_FORCE_<EXPERIMENT_NAME_UPPER>` from env

**`ExperimentServiceTest.java`** — 14 tests covering: disabled returns control, unknown returns null-value control, `controlWeight=1.0` always control (200 iterations), `controlWeight=0.0` always treatment, env-var forces control/treatment override, `getActiveAssignments` filtering, `isEnabled` for non-existent experiment, `readForceOverride` returns null when env var absent.

### Files Modified

**`application.yml`** — Added `ai.experiments.experiments` block with three entries: `embedding-model`, `llm-model`, `hyde` — all `enabled: false`, `control-weight: 1.0`.

content.thinking:
**Assessing potential material defects**

I need to assess if there’s any material defect in the work. There's a potential issue because the spec said to create two new files, but they made three, including ExperimentAssignment. While that might seem harmless, perhaps the parent orchestrator might not appreciate the extra file? I need to consider whether assignments should be deterministic per user. The description mentions a config-driven variant assignment, but it looks like the userId is mainly used for future purposes.

**Reviewing configuration and potential issues**

I should check the config path in application.yml. The double "experiments" seems odd, but it matches the class prefix and is likely fine. I need to see if there are tests that validate application binding — they're possibly absent, which could be alright. I'm also considering whether the Spring Boot starter has configuration properties processing enabled. There are some contradictory docs about `assign` and `null-checks`, but they don’t seem material. I’ll need to make sure the env var override is functioning as specified before approving, especially since checks are passing.

**Considering observations and potential risks**

I’m thinking about mentioning the extra file; it’s low priority, but it seems harmless. Parent conversation handoff summary
Summary model: openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini
Source items: 73

Overview:
The discussion centered on whether LightRAG-style simplification would fit this repo’s AI stack, then shifted into a broader AI pipeline optimization effort. The repo already has a fairly advanced RAG/AI architecture: Spring Boot backend, Pinecone vector search, prompt templates, eval harnesses, and derived read models. The resulting direction was to keep a reusable experiment framework, on-demand A/B evaluation, and a few quality/cost improvements, all with backward-compatible defaults.

Settled decisions:
- Proceed with a reusable live experiment framework, not just one-off eval scripts.
- Keep experiments default-off so current behavior is unchanged unless explicitly enabled.
- Use OpenRouter as the sole LLM gateway; do not add direct provider calls.
- Use existing eval datasets/judging infrastructure for on-demand A/B comparisons.
- Record experiment/model metadata in existing audit-related fields rather than adding schema migrations.
- Add prompt caching support for Anthropic models via OpenRouter.
- Add SQL routing for analytical questions to use read models instead of vector search.
- Keep HyDE as an opt-in, experiment-controlled RAG enhancement.
- Add chunk-size monitoring/analysis as a diagnostic/observability improvement.
- A plan file was written: `duet-plan-ai-optimization.md` for duet-based implementation.

Constraints / must-remember items:
- Implementation should stay backend-only for this plan; no frontend/package/e2e changes.
- Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.4 stack; avoid introducing new framework dependencies.
- All new behaviors must be backward compatible and disabled by default.
- OpenRouter remains the only model gateway.
- Existing eval tests must continue to pass.
- The plan assumes no database schema changes.
- The repo already contains derived read models relevant to SQL routing: UserWeekFact, TeamWeekRollup, RcdoWeekRollup, CarryForwardFact, ComplianceFact.
- Duet config in `.pi/duet/config.json` shows static checks: `npm run check:static`; unit checks: `npm test`.
- There is already an in-progress duet run with modified files under `.pi/duet/runs/2026-03-29T18-32-40-613Z-32faa966/` and tracked changes in `AiController` plus new plan recommendation files.

Open questions:
- Should the initial implementation keep the full live traffic experiment service, or trim it down later if it proves too heavy?
- Which exact models should be treated as control/treatment defaults for LLM and embeddings in the reusable A/B harness?
- Should analytical SQL routing be limited to clearly aggregate/comparison queries, or should it also handle broader reporting-style questions?
- How much observability is wanted for prompt caching and chunk-size analysis beyond basic logging/counters?
- Should the A/B runner compare only LLMs initially, or also embeddings and HyDE in the same reusable harness?

Use this handoff as background context. If it conflicts with the explicit task below or the current repository state, prioritize the explicit task and repo state.

  "goal": "Add seven optimization capabilities to the AI pipeline — prompt caching, A/B testing infrastructure, embedding model override (offline eval only), LLM model A/B, HyDE, SQL query routing, and chunk size analysis — all backward-compatible and default-off. The embedding-model A/B experiment is scoped to offline evaluation only (via AbComparisonRunner) because the Pinecone index is fixed at 1536 dimensions matching text-embedding-3-small; routing live queries through text-embedding-3-large (3072-d) would cause dimension mismatches. Backend-only; no frontend, e2e, or schema changes.",

Add int getDimensions(String model) returning 1536 for text-embedding-3-small, 3072 for text-embedding-3-large (for callers that need to know vector size). Add per-model AtomicLong call counters. IMPORTANT: The embed(text, modelOverride) method is intended for offline evaluation (AbComparisonRunner) only — it must NOT be used in the live SemanticQueryService query path because the Pinecone index stores 1536-d vectors and querying with 3072-d vectors would fail. (3) Modify backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticIndexService.java — inject ChunkSizeAnalyzer via constructor (adding 15th param to existing 14-param constructor), call chunkSizeAnalyzer.analyze(chunk) and chunkSizeAnalyzer.logWarningIfOversized(chunk) in the upsertChunk() method after building the chunk. Create tests: backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkSizeAnalyzerTest.java (token estimation, threshold boundary, metrics recording). All 22 tests pass. Here's a summary of everything done:

## Summary

### `EmbeddingService.java` (modified)
- Added `ConcurrentHashMap<String, AtomicLong> modelCallCounters` for per-model observability.
- Refactored `embed(String text)` to delegate to the new `embed(String text, String modelOverride)`.
- New `embed(String text, String modelOverride)` implements the full HTTP logic with `effectiveModel` (falls back to `this.model` if override is null/blank); increments the per-model counter on successful parse.
- New `getDimensions(String model)` returns 3072 for `openai/text-embedding-3-large`, 1536 for everything else (including null).
- New `getCallCount(String model)` returns the accumulated success count for any model key.

### `ChunkSizeAnalyzer.java` (new)
- `@Component` taking `MeterRegistry` in constructor, registers `DistributionSummary("ai.chunk.tokens")`.
- `analyze(ChunkData)` estimates tokens (chars / 4), records into the summary, returns `ChunkStats` record with `estimatedTokens`, `charLength`, `metadataKeyCount`, `exceedsRecommendedSize` (threshold: 625 tokens).
- `logWarningIfOversized(ChunkData)` logs `WARN` when `estimatedTokens > 625`.

### `SemanticIndexService.java` (modified)
- Added `ChunkSizeAnalyzer chunkSizeAnalyzer` field and 15th constructor parameter.
- `upsertChunk()` now calls `chunkSizeAnalyzer.analyze(chunk)` then `chunkSizeAnalyzer.logWarningIfOversized(chunk)` before embedding.

### `ChunkSizeAnalyzerTest.java` (new) — 13 tests
- Token estimation (empty, boundary, null text), threshold boundary (at/above/below 625), metadata key count, metrics recording (single and accumulated), `logWarningIfOversized` smoke tests.

### `EmbeddingServiceTest.java` (modified) — 10 new tests
- `embed(text, modelOverride)` uses override in request body; null/blank override falls back to default.
- Call counter increments on success, not on failure; `getCallCount` returns 0 for unknown model.
- `getDimensions` for small (1536), large (3072), unknown (1536), null (1536).

content.thinking:
**Inspecting potential issues**

I need to inspect the ChunkSizeAnalyzer for any differences, and there's maybe a new file with no diff. I should test and compile it to ensure it passes. I'm considering material defects and analyzing estimates of tokens calculated via floor chars/4. The description mentions estimating tokens around four characters per token, which should usually be chars/4. The threshold is 625, so at 2501 chars, it seems fine. However, if using approximations, I should be cautious about underestimating since the step doesn't specify rounding. There’s also a change in the SemanticIndexService constructor that may need attention for direct instantiation. I've noticed additional potential computation double counts and the use of AtomicLong for per-model counters.

**Verifying potential changes**

I'm concerned that "IMPORTANT" not being used in the live SemanticQueryService query path could be an issue, so I need to verify that no accidental changes were made. It's possible this verification isn't necessary, but I wonder if I can check for misuse, especially since the new method might not be used yet. It seems that the service's embed(String text) now delegates to embed(text, this.model), which should be fine. I should check if there are any calls to the new overload beyond tests, so I'll grep for "embed(" with a comma to see what turns up.

Reviewed the step-3 diff in:

- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/EmbeddingService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkSizeAnalyzer.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticIndexService.java`
- `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/EmbeddingServiceTest.java`
- `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkSizeAnalyzerTest.java`

What I verified:
- `EmbeddingService` now has:
  - `embed(String text, String modelOverride)`
  - existing `embed(String text)` delegating to the configured model
  - `getDimensions(String model)`
  - per-model call counters
- The override path is not used anywhere in main code outside `EmbeddingService` itself, so the live query path is still not routing through a model override.
- `ChunkSizeAnalyzer` exists as a component, records `ai.chunk.tokens`, returns `ChunkStats`, and warns for oversized chunks.
- `SemanticIndexService` injects `ChunkSizeAnalyzer` and invokes it in `upsertChunk()`.
- Added/updated tests cover the new embedding override behavior, dimensions, counters, token estimation, threshold handling, and metrics recording.

Checks:
- `npm run check:static` ✅
- `npm test` ✅

Assessment:
- The step requirements are met.
- I did not find a material defect in the prior changes.
- No changes were needed.

```observations
- [low] frontend/src/components/myweek/PlanHistoryView.tsx: Existing test output still reports a missing React `key` warning.
- [low] frontend/src/components/notifications/NotificationPanel.tsx: Existing test output still reports repeated React `act(...)` warnings.
```

Verdict: approve

Parent conversation handoff summary:
Parent conversation handoff summary
Summary model: openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini
Source items: 73

Overview:
The discussion centered on whether LightRAG-style simplification would fit this repo’s AI stack, then shifted into a broader AI pipeline optimization effort. The repo already has a fairly advanced RAG/AI architecture: Spring Boot backend, Pinecone vector search, prompt templates, eval harnesses, and derived read models. The resulting direction was to keep a reusable experiment framework, on-demand A/B evaluation, and a few quality/cost improvements, all with backward-compatible defaults.

Settled decisions:
- Proceed with a reusable live experiment framework, not just one-off eval scripts.
- Keep experiments default-off so current behavior is unchanged unless explicitly enabled.
- Use OpenRouter as the sole LLM gateway; do not add direct provider calls.
- Use existing eval datasets/judging infrastructure for on-demand A/B comparisons.
- Record experiment/model metadata in existing audit-related fields rather than adding schema migrations.
- Add prompt caching support for Anthropic models via OpenRouter.
- Add SQL routing for analytical questions to use read models instead of vector search.
- Keep HyDE as an opt-in, experiment-controlled RAG enhancement.
- Add chunk-size monitoring/analysis as a diagnostic/observability improvement.
- A plan file was written: `duet-plan-ai-optimization.md` for duet-based implementation.

Constraints / must-remember items:
- Implementation should stay backend-only for this plan; no frontend/package/e2e changes.
- Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.4 stack; avoid introducing new framework dependencies.
- All new behaviors must be backward compatible and disabled by default.
- OpenRouter remains the only model gateway.
- Existing eval tests must continue to pass.
- The plan assumes no database schema changes.
- The repo already contains derived read models relevant to SQL routing: UserWeekFact, TeamWeekRollup, RcdoWeekRollup, CarryForwardFact, ComplianceFact.
- Duet config in `.pi/duet/config.json` shows static checks: `npm run check:static`; unit checks: `npm test`.
- There is already an in-progress duet run with modified files under `.pi/duet/runs/2026-03-29T18-32-40-613Z-32faa966/` and tracked changes in `AiController` plus new plan recommendation files.

Open questions:
- Should the initial implementation keep the full live traffic experiment service, or trim it down later if it proves too heavy?
- Which exact models should be treated as control/treatment defaults for LLM and embeddings in the reusable A/B harness?
- Should analytical SQL routing be limited to clearly aggregate/comparison queries, or should it also handle broader reporting-style questions?
- How much observability is wanted for prompt caching and chunk-size analysis beyond basic logging/counters?
- Should the A/B runner compare only LLMs initially, or also embeddings and HyDE in the same reusable harness?

Use this handoff as background context. If it conflicts with the explicit task below or the current repository state, prioritize the explicit task and repo state.

Plan overview:
{
  "goal": "Add seven optimization capabilities to the AI pipeline — prompt caching, A/B testing infrastructure, embedding model override (offline eval only), LLM model A/B, HyDE, SQL query routing, and chunk size analysis — all backward-compatible and default-off. The embedding-model A/B experiment is scoped to offline evaluation only (via AbComparisonRunner) because the Pinecone index is fixed at 1536 dimensions matching text-embedding-3-small; routing live queries through text-embedding-3-large (3072-d) would cause dimension mismatches. Backend-only; no frontend, e2e, or schema changes.",
  "totalSteps": 7,
  "stepTitles": [
    "1. Prompt Caching in OpenRouterAiProvider",
    "2. A/B Testing Infrastructure",
    "3. Embedding Model Override (Offline Eval) + Chunk Size Analysis",
    "4. LLM Model A/B + Experiment-Aware Provider",
    "5. HyDE Service + SQL Query Router",
    "6. Wire HyDE, SQL Routing, and LLM A/B into SemanticQueryService",
    "7. A/B Comparison Eval Runner"
  ]
}

Step 4: LLM Model A/B + Experiment-Aware Provider
{
  "id": "step-4",
  "title": "LLM Model A/B + Experiment-Aware Provider",
  "description": "Modify the AI provider chain to support per-request model override driven by experiments. (1) Modify backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiProvider.java — add default method: default AiSuggestionResult generateSuggestion(AiContext context, String modelOverride) { return generateSuggestion(context); } so StubAiProvider inherits it unchanged. (2) Modify backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiSuggestionResult.java — add 7th field Map<String, String> experimentAssignments to the record; add backward-compatible constructors: existing 6-param and 5-param delegate to canonical 7-param with Map.of(); update unavailable() factory. (3) Modify backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/OpenRouterAiProvider.java — override generateSuggestion(AiContext, String modelOverride); refactor callOpenRouter() to accept String effectiveModel parameter (when non-null, use it instead of this.model); when effectiveModel does NOT start with \"anthropic/\", do NOT add cache_control; update parseResponse to use the effective model in the result's modelVersion. The original generateSuggestion(AiContext) delegates with null. (4) Modify backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiProviderRegistry.java — inject ExperimentService (nullable, using @Autowired(required=false) or null-safe constructor); add generateSuggestion(AiContext, UUID userId) that checks llm-model experiment, resolves variant, calls provider.generateSuggestion(context, modelOverride), attaches experiment assignments. Original generateSuggestion(AiContext) unchanged. Update tests: backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/OpenRouterAiProviderTest.java (model override in parseResponse), backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiProviderRegistryTest.java (update constructor, add experiment-aware test).",
  "requiredChecks": [
    "static",
    "unit"
  ]
}

Previous agent's notes:
All 22 JS tests and all Java tests pass. Both static and unit checks are green.

## Summary of changes

### `AiProvider.java`
Added `default generateSuggestion(AiContext, String modelOverride)` that delegates to `generateSuggestion(context)`. `StubAiProvider` inherits this unchanged.

### `AiSuggestionResult.java`
Added 7th field `Map<String, String> experimentAssignments` to the record. Added backward-compatible 6-param constructor (adds `Map.of()`) and kept the existing 5-param constructor (adds both null promptVersion and `Map.of()`). Updated `unavailable()` factory to pass `Map.of()`.

### `OpenRouterAiProvider.java`
- `generateSuggestion(AiContext)` now delegates to `generateSuggestion(context, null)`.
- New `@Override generateSuggestion(AiContext, String modelOverride)` resolves `effectiveModel` (override or default), passes it to `callOpenRouter` and `parseResponse`.
- `callOpenRouter(systemPrompt, userMessage, effectiveModel)` — uses `effectiveModel` for the request body `model` field and for the `cache_control` Anthropic check.
- `parseResponse` gets a 4-param overload `(body, type, promptVersion, effectiveModel)` that uses `effectiveModel` for `modelVersion` in the result. The existing 3-param overload delegates to it with `this.model`.

### `AiProviderRegistry.java`
- `@Autowired(required=false) ExperimentService experimentService` field.
- New `generateSuggestion(AiContext, UUID userId)` — checks `llm-model` experiment, passes model override on treatment, records `experimentAssignments` in the result.
- `setExperimentService(ExperimentService)` for test wiring.

### Tests
- `OpenRouterAiProviderTest`: added `parseResponse_withModelOverride_usesOverrideForModelVersion` and `parseResponse_threeParamOverload_usesProviderModel`.
- `AiProviderRegistryTest`: added `ExperimentService` mock, and 5 new experiment-aware tests covering: no service wired, control variant, treatment variant, AI disabled, provider throws.

Verdict: changes_made

content.thinking:
**Deciding on approval**

I need to decide whether to approve this. I should mention that the registry isn't checking if the experiment is enabled. I should look at the ExperimentService documentation again to understand the semantics of using `getActiveAssignments` versus assignments that don’t return control. While I could note the possibility of non-deterministic assignment, I'll focus on observing the disabled experiment metadata. This looks like a probable bug with medium severity. I need to think carefully about whether to make this change.

**Identifying a concrete defect**

It seems there's a concrete defect with the `generateSuggestion(context, userId)` method. It's attaching the `llm-model` control assignment even when the experiment is disabled, which contradicts the "default-off" principle. This could cause differing outputs if the method is called with experiments off, potentially affecting the persisted audit. It looks like there should be a check for the `llm-model experiment` using `isEnabled` instead. I need to confirm if there are any current callers of this method in the repository. A `grep` search shows none, so the method's semantics still need to match upcoming changes.

**Deciding on method fixes**

I need to ensure this support method aligns with expectations. First, I must determine if I should fix this issue now. It's a small targeted fix along with a test, which I think is manageable, so let's go for it. I need to update `AiProviderRegistry` and modify `generateSuggestion(context, userId)` to assign only if `experimentService` is not null and if `isEnabled("llm-model")` returns true. I should also add a test to confirm that when the experiment is disabled, it returns the base result and has no experiment assignments.

content.thinking:
**Documenting changes made**

I need to mention the changes I've made in a concise way. I've reviewed the differences and fixed a material issue, so now the registry only consults or records LLM-model assignments when the experiment is enabled, maintaining the default-off semantics. I added a test as well. Observations should probably still show the same frontend warnings, perhaps including the MockBean deprecation, but this might be outside the scope. content.text:
Reviewed the step-4 diff in:

- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiProvider.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiSuggestionResult.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/OpenRouterAiProvider.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiProviderRegistry.java`
- `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/OpenRouterAiProviderTest.java`
- `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiProviderRegistryTest.java`

What I found:
- Most of the step was implemented correctly.
- I found one material issue in `AiProviderRegistry`: it was recording `llm-model` experiment assignments whenever `ExperimentService` was present, even if the experiment was disabled. That breaks the “default-off / unchanged unless enabled” requirement.

What I changed:
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiProviderRegistry.java`
  - gated experiment resolution on `experimentService.isEnabled("llm-model")`
- `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiProviderRegistryTest.java`
  - updated existing experiment-aware tests to stub `isEnabled("llm-model")`
  - added coverage for the disabled-experiment case to ensure no assignment is attached and no override is passed

Checks:
- `npm run check:static` ✅
- `npm test` ✅

Assessment:
- After the fix, the step requirements are met and the default-off behavior is preserved.

```observations
- [low] frontend/src/components/myweek/PlanHistoryView.tsx: Existing test output still reports a missing React `key` warning.
- [low] frontend/src/components/notifications/NotificationPanel.tsx: Existing test output still reports repeated React `act(...)` warnings.
- [low] backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/controller/AiControllerTest.java: Backend test compilation emits Spring `@MockBean` deprecation warnings.
```

Verdict: changes_made
